mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior month signals easier financial conditions and a lower equity discount rate, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is still elevated, real financing costs remain restrictive and equity multiples compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the sign of the interaction term is not negative in rolling estimation.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent level indicates weakening household income and demand, which should depress risk appetite and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp versus 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon returns are not negative in triggered months or if the triggered sample shows positive 5-day returns more often than negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS growth combined with a stable or falling UNRATE signals broad labor-market expansion, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth and should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with positive PAYEMS momentum and non-rising UNRATE do not deliver above-baseline 60-day returns, or if the interaction adds no predictive value versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in RSAFS above a normal sales trend indicates stronger final demand and earnings momentum, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-RSAFS-growth months fail to outperform the unconditional 20-day return distribution, or if the hit rate is below 55% after transaction-neutral evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST after a prior buildup signals weakening housing demand and a drag on cyclical growth, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month HOUST declines are not followed by below-average 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal flips sign in more than one major rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in INDPRO and PAYEMS indicates rising output with labor utilization, supporting earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in INDPRO and PAYEMS do not outperform single-series signals, or if 20-day forward returns are not positive in a majority of joint-expansion observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, rising GDPC1 combined with easing CPIAUCSL reflects real growth without renewed inflation pressure, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with rising real GDP and falling inflation do not produce above-baseline 60-day equity returns, or if the joint condition has no incremental predictive power versus GDPC1 alone.
